review_notes: Direct review says this is still a segmented opaque bundle case. The current evidence does not justify prioritizing cmd-injection over bundle/container reconstruction.
Direct review says this is still a segmented opaque bundle case. The current evidence does not justify prioritizing cmd-injection over bundle/container reconstruction.
Direct review agrees this sample is still an opaque encrypted container. The current evidence supports container-analysis and decrypt-probe follow-up, not web-level vulnerability triage.
Direct review agrees this sample is still an opaque encrypted container. The current evidence supports container-analysis and decrypt-probe follow-up, not web-level vulnerability triage.
Direct review agrees this sample is still a cloud-container recovery case. The current evidence supports payload inspection and vendor updater reversing before any web-risk triage.

Direct review of the `system.lua` candidate shows fixed maintenance actions around upgrade/reset/backup and `/bin/sh`, but not a mapped attacker-controlled shell fragment. Treat this as upgrade/maintenance risk, not command injection.

Direct review of `firmware.lua` shows firmware/upgrade status handling, fixed process-inspection pipelines, and status-file writes. The current evidence does not map an attacker-controlled shell fragment into the pipeline string, so this should be treated as upgrade/maintenance risk rather than command injection.

Direct review of `pc_schedule` shows a local shell-script variable-injection pattern with no surfaced web endpoint and no demonstrated web reachability. Keep it as local script risk/noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Direct review of `wifi_check_country` shows a local shell-variable pattern with no surfaced web endpoint and no concrete web reachability. Keep it as local script noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Direct review of `unit_test.sh` shows a local shell-script download helper with no surfaced web endpoint and no demonstrated web reachability. Keep it as local script noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Direct review of `arp_protection` shows a local CLI/network utility (`usage: arpprotection [count] [ifname]`) with generic exec/system/popen helpers but no surfaced web endpoint or demonstrated HTTP dispatch. Keep it as local utility noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Direct review of `arp_protection` shows a local CLI/network utility (`usage: arpprotection [count] [ifname]`) with generic exec/system/popen helpers but no surfaced web endpoint or demonstrated HTTP dispatch. Keep it as local utility noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Direct review of `arp_protection` shows a local CLI/network utility (`usage: arpprotection [count] [ifname]`) with generic exec/system/popen helpers but no surfaced web endpoint or demonstrated HTTP dispatch. Keep it as local utility noise rather than current web-RCE triage.

Existing AX3000M diagnostic review confirms a hidden `/cgibin/d.cgi` dispatcher inside `timepro.cgi` for 14.234, gated by auth/csrf plus the non-public `aaksjdkfj` parameter before `cmd -> popen()`. Treat this as a confirmed hidden diagnostic command-execution case, not as generic `chgrp` noise.

Existing AX3000M diagnostic review confirms standalone `d.cgi` in 15.024 with auth/csrf plus the non-public `aaksjdkfj` gate before `cmd -> popen()`. Treat this as a confirmed hidden diagnostic command-execution case rather than local helper noise.

Existing X6000R mtkwifi review confirms `submit_dpp_uri()` directly concatenates `uri` into `os.execute()` and the same controller also exposes `apcli_cfg -> apcli_connect` command-substitution injection. Keep this as a confirmed command-injection case.

Existing X6000R mtkwifi review confirms `submit_dpp_uri()` directly concatenates `uri` into `os.execute()` and the same controller also exposes `apcli_cfg -> apcli_connect` command-substitution injection. Keep this as a confirmed command-injection case.
The older X6000R build shows the same `mtkwifi.lua/submit_dpp_uri` controller pattern and should be treated as the same confirmed command-injection family pending finer version-diff work.

`boa/formUploadFile` remains a strong confirmed post-auth web command-injection path through `formUploadConfig -> /bin/sh`. Keep this as a real command-execution case rather than downgrading it into generic maintenance behavior.

`boa/formUploadFile` remains a strong confirmed post-auth web command-injection path through `formUploadConfig -> /bin/sh`. Keep this as a real command-execution case rather than downgrading it into generic maintenance behavior.
The older A3002RU 20201208 build shows the same `boa/formUploadFile -> formUploadConfig -> /bin/sh` command-construction family and should be kept as a confirmed web command-injection case.
The later A3002RU 20230809 build still surfaces the same `boa/formUploadFile -> formUploadConfig -> /bin/sh` command-construction family and should remain classified as confirmed command injection.

Existing Deco XE75 review shows the stronger issue is a hardcoded RSA-512 mesh group private key in `group-info`, which enables mesh-auth bypass, unsigned firmware flash, and full-mesh pivoting. Treat this sample primarily as a crypto/authentication failure, not as speculative `ndppd` command injection.

Existing Deco XE75/XE5300/WE10800 review shows the stronger issue is a hardcoded RSA-512 mesh group private key in `group-info`, enabling mesh-auth bypass and unsigned firmware propagation. Keep this family under crypto/authentication risk rather than speculative `ndppd` command injection.

Direct review of MR90X shows the stronger family is structural memory-corruption in exposed service binaries (`uhttpd`, `miniupnpd`), not a mapped command-injection chain. Keep this sample under overflow/memory-corruption triage until a concrete parameter-to-sink proof exists.
